title: agropyron repens subsp. campestre
intro: Agropyron repens subsp. campestre, also known as creeping bentgrass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of creeping bentgrass are often consumed as a tea or used in infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
